text:
# `mxlpy.fit.losses`

Loss functions.

## Functions

### `cosine_similarity(y_pred: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series, y_true: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series) -> float`

Calculate negative cosine similarity between model and data.

Parameters
----------
y_pred
    Predicted values.
y_true
    True (observed) values.

Returns
-------
float
    Negative cosine similarity.

### `mae(y_pred: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series, y_true: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series) -> float`

Calculate mean absolute error.

Parameters
----------
y_pred
    Predicted values.
y_true
    True (observed) values.

Returns
-------
float
    Mean absolute error.

### `mean(y_pred: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series, y_true: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series) -> float`

Calculate mean error between model and data.

Parameters
----------
y_pred
    Predicted values.
y_true
    True (observed) values.

Returns
-------
float
    Mean error.

### `mean_absolute_percentage(y_pred: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series, y_true: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series) -> float`

Calculate mean absolute percentage error.

Parameters
----------
y_pred
    Predicted values.
y_true
    True (observed) values.

Returns
-------
float
    Mean absolute percentage error.

### `mean_squared(y_pred: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series, y_true: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series) -> float`

Calculate mean squared error between model and data.

Parameters
----------
y_pred
    Predicted values.
y_true
    True (observed) values.

Returns
-------
float
    Mean squared error.

### `mean_squared_logarithmic(y_pred: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series, y_true: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series) -> float`

Calculate mean squared logarithmic error between model and data.

Parameters
----------
y_pred
    Predicted values.
y_true
    True (observed) values.

Returns
-------
float
    Mean squared logarithmic error.

### `rmse(y_pred: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series, y_true: pd.DataFrame | pd.Series) -> float`

Calculate root mean square error between model and data.

Parameters
----------
y_pred
    Predicted values.
y_true
    True (observed) values.

Returns
-------
float
    Root mean square error.
